name: Sakina
title: Beloved of Imam Hussain
shortDescription: The dear daughter of Imam Hussain (AS) and Rabab who was present in Karbala and proclaimed her father's innocence with her heart-rending elegies.
fullStory:
Hazrat Sakina (Amina) was the beloved and noble daughter of Imam Hussain (AS) and Rabab. She was a very pious, eloquent, and literary girl whom Imam Hussain (AS) described by saying: 'Sakina is mostly absorbed in the essence of God.'

During the event of Karbala, Sakina was a young teenager (around ten to thirteen years old). She witnessed the martyrdom of all her brothers, uncles, and finally her dear father. The bitter farewell of Imam Hussain (AS) to Sakina on Ashura is one of the most heart-rending moments of Karbala; when the Imam pressed her to his chest and wiped her tears.

After Ashura, Sakina was taken captive along with her aunt Lady Zaynab (SA) and her mother Rabab. On the journey to Kufa and Sham, despite all the hardships and lashings, she behaved with dignity and chastity, acting as a messenger of the Karbala martyrs' innocence.

One of the painful accounts is when Sakina stood by her father's headless body and embraced it, to the point that Umar Sa'd's soldiers had to forcefully separate her from her father's body.

After returning to Medina, Hazrat Sakina kept the Karbala tragedy alive for years by holding mourning gatherings and reciting elegiac poetry. She passed away in Medina in 117 AH.
quoteArabic: شيعَتي ما إِنْ شَرِبْتُمْ ماءَ عَذْبٍ فَاذْكُرُوني...
quote: (A poem she recited on behalf of her father:) My Shias, whenever you drink sweet water, remember me...
